data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding; findings list EMPTY.

fe57 is the FOUNDATION story (the fixtures + FakeAcliClient + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc). Its consumers — 8937 (snapshot contract), 59ef (verified-fake fidelity), 822a (bidirectional round-trip) — each declare depends_on fe57 and consume its fixtures/fake. Repo confirms the as-shipped fake lives at tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py with the four read-only enrichment methods (search_issues / get_comment_map / get_issuelinks_map / get_parent_map), served through fetcher.fetch_snapshot with no shape massaging.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57 PRODUCES _fakes.py + tests/fixtures/jira/ + scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py + re-capture doc; siblings CONSUME them. Clean producer/consumer split over the declared depends_on fe57 edges; no sibling co-edits fe57's files (8937→_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py; 59ef→test_verified_fake_contract.py/_jira_shape_contract.py, acli_graph.py read-only; 822a→test_roundtrip.py/probe script/docs; 33d0/2359→outbound applier cluster). No shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves the fixtures through the production fetcher enrichment path with NO shape transformation") is exactly what every consumer assumes: 8937's snapshot-entry schema targets the unmassaged comment(nested)/issuelinks/parent shapes the fake feeds through fetch_snapshot; 59ef's dual-run contract requires precisely an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake to pass against the real client; 822a's inbound leg drives the same enrichment merge. The fake's read-only enrichment surface matches the inbound/enrichment surface all three consume. (822a's outbound leg uses a separate recording write-client — that is 822a's own in-scope mechanism; 822a does not assert fe57's fake must expose an apply surface, so there is no contradiction with fe57's stated deliverable.) The epic's one real representation conflict (outbound labels_applied list-vs-int, localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing) does not touch fe57's inbound fixtures/fake.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the foundation with no upstream dependency; correctly sequenced FIRST per the epic's MVP-first slice (A→B on the known comment/issuelinks seam). Its In/Out scope is consistent: it OUTs the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef), both present as named consumers that declare depends_on fe57. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc; 59ef owns the real-vs-fake fidelity VERIFICATION of that fake; 8937 owns the schema SSOT + producer→consumer round-trip that CONSUMES the fixtures; 822a owns the bidirectional probe. Complementary build-vs-verify-vs-consume split, not duplicative. The secret-scrubbing/re-capture deliverable is uniquely fe57's.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. fe57 lands first; all three consumers are gated by the declared depends_on fe57 edge. fe57's files are entirely disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. fe57's read-only fake surface is SUFFICIENT for its consumers' needs as scoped: 8937 and 59ef exercise only the inbound enrichment surface; 822a's inbound leg uses it and sources its outbound leg separately (in 822a's scope). fe57 imposes no brittle expectation — fixtures are real captured payloads served unmassaged, and the re-capture doc explicitly guards against fixtures rotting into change-detectors (consistent with parent SC "zero change-detector / lean suite").
- Residual references — BENIGN. The one candidate is a path-naming discrepancy: fe57's prose names the fake at tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py, whereas the as-shipped code and ALL consuming siblings (8937/59ef/822a) consistently use tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py. Because the producer's shipped artifact and every consumer agree on the jira_contract/ location, nothing is stranded — this is a benign prose-vs-shipped path nuance, not a high-confidence cross-child residual-reference inconsistency. All four enrichment methods fe57's fake exposes are live on the real client and not moved/renamed by any sibling; 33d0's deletion targets the OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier, not fe57's inbound enrichment surface, so it strands nothing fe57 produces.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (and per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list EMPTY.
